steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence has two parts that must be separated: (1) "Bear River High School and Bryce Valley High School are two of several Utah schools [that have their own hillside geoglyphs]" and (2) "An enormous geoglyph of the letters BR overlooks Bear River High, while a geoglyph of the letters BV overlooks Bryce Valley High." Part 2 is a full independent clause, so part 1 must also end cleanly. The relative clause identifying which schools is restrictive (no commas), and the two independent clauses need a hard stop between them.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "schools, that have their own hillside geoglyphs, and an": Puts commas around "that have their own hillside geoglyphs," making it nonrestrictive — wrong because it specifies which schools. Also uses "and an" to join the two clauses, forming a run-on without a proper stop. ✗
  • Choice B — "schools that have their own hillside geoglyphs. An": No comma before "that" — correct for a restrictive clause. A period after "geoglyphs" properly separates the two independent clauses. ✓
  • Choice C — "schools, that have their own hillside geoglyphs and an": Incorrectly places a comma before the restrictive "that" clause and uses "and an" without a period, creating a run-on. ✗
  • Choice D — "schools that have their own hillside geoglyphs, an": Correct restrictive clause but joins the two independent clauses with only a comma — a comma splice. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
